steps.title: Identify the scale factor between similar rectangles
steps.content: Rectangle EFGH is similar to rectangle ABCD, with each side of EFGH being 7 times the corresponding side of ABCD. The linear scale factor is $k = 7$.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule for similar figures
steps.content: For similar figures with a linear scale factor of $k$, the area scale factor is $k^2$. Since $k = 7$, the area of EFGH is $7^2 = 49$ times the area of ABCD.
